game_name: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles IV: Turtles in Time

design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate horizontally through themed levels, defeat waves of enemies and bosses, progress through time periods via narrative time-warp mechanic
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Managing health/lives while juggling multiple aggressive enemies; boss pattern recognition and execution
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Play as iconic TMNT characters, utilize character-specific strengths/weaknesses, engage in visceral combat across historical eras and fantastical settings
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Character differentiation (each turtle has distinct strengths/weaknesses), time-travel narrative framing for level design, power-attack combo system rewarding sustained offense, ability to weaponize enemy positioning (slamming into others)

difficulty.lives_system: Configurable via options menu; North American/European versions allow up to 7 lives selectable; Japanese version reduced maximum to 3 lives
difficulty.continues_system: Configurable; North American/European versions allow up to 5 continues tied to difficulty level; Japanese version reduced maximum to 3 continues, decoupled from difficulty
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Player can pre-select difficulty level which affects continue availability in international versions; lives and continues are menu-configurable before play

progression.unlock_mechanism: Hidden level select accessible via Game Genie code (US version: 26C4-640F) or button combination on second controller (Japanese version: Up, Up, Down, Down, Left, Right, Left, Right, L, R)
replayability_hooks: Time Trial mode exclusive to SNES version
Versus mode exclusive to SNES version
Character selection affecting available strategies per playthrough
Level select for practicing specific encounters
Different boss lineups between arcade and SNES versions

world_building_techniques: Introductory cutscene establishing high-concept premise (Statue of Liberty theft, time-warp imprisonment)
Level theming tied to historical/fantastical eras (past, future, technodrome)
Boss character selection drawing from 1987 cartoon and film universe
Post-defeat boss dialogue in Japanese version providing minimal character moments (7 of 9 bosses have spoken reactions)

boss_design.philosophy: Boss roster mixes recurring TMNT villains with form-specific variants (Krang in UFO vs. Super Krang); arcade version features 8 bosses across varied enemy archetypes; SNES version adds Technodrome level with restructured boss sequence

faction_indicators: Foot Clan soldiers as primary enemy type
Shredder as antagonist leader commanding army
danger_signals: Statue of Liberty theft signals escalating threat
Time-warp trap indicates advanced faction capability
Diverse boss roster suggests organized military structure
environmental_storytelling: New York City streets establish familiar contemporary setting before temporal displacement
Historical/future level theming communicates faction's temporal manipulation technology
Technodrome environment (SNES exclusive) grounds final confrontation in faction headquarters

sources_used: The Cutting Room Floor - TMNT IV article (regional differences, game genie codes, boss dialogue, lives/continues configuration)
Wikipedia - Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles: Turtles in Time (gameplay mechanics, boss roster, plot summary, development, SNES-exclusive features)
